violations.text: Inspector observed shellstock (oysters) delivered at 51DF.Per observation on arrival inspector observed shellstock bag being stored on floor of delivery truck.PIC voluntarily disposed of shellstock. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (slicer) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The chlorine sanitizer concentration for the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher was (0) parts per million (ppm). Chlorine sanitizing solutions must be between 50-100 ppm. Employee corrected during inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026
(Food product ) were observed stored on the floor in the (storage area ). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026
Hand towel observed being stored over pasta on cookline prep unit.PIC informed to cease practice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The non-food contact surfaces of the (storage shelves in server area and euipment(storage area) ) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026
handsink observed in poor repair. Observed leak at plumbing. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026

The floors and walls throughout the kitchen and food storage area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 04/01/2026
Inspector observed ROP cooked octopus processed in the establishment. PIC informed to cease practice.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025

(Smelts ) was observed thawing in a bucket of water on the counter. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods must be thawed in one of the following manners: under refrigeration (41 degrees Fahrenheit or less); completely submerged under running water; as part of the cooking process; or in the microwave if cooked immediately after thawing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/20/2025

An employee was observed washing their hands at the (food prep) sink. Food employees shall clean their hands in a designated hand-washing sink and not in a food preparation, mop, or ware-washing sink. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025
Utensils and towels were observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025
Molluscan Shellfish tags did not have all of the required information.(Dates of last used) were lacking. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025

Raw animal foods were not stored properly to prevent cross-contamination. Except when combined as ingredients, different types of raw animal foods must be separated from each other during storage by using separate equipment or arranging each type of food in equipment so that cross contamination of one type with another is prevented.PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener and serving spoons) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. PIC moved to warewashing area to be washed and sanitized. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025

(Baked potatoes) was observed held at (50 ) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be maintained at 135 degrees Fahrenheit or above except during preparation, cooking, or cooling. PIC reheated. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025

(Pot Roast and garlic and oil) exceeding the 7 day datemarking. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation.PIC voluntarily disposed of product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025
(Various food product ) were observed stored on the floor in the (walk in cooler ). Food must be stored at least 6 inches above the floor. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the (food storage shelves ) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025

The floors and walls in kitchen and back food storage area have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/05/2025
A knife was observed in the hand-washing sink. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

The presence of (rodents) is not controlled as evidenced by (droppings in office ). The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 06/12/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
The hand-washing sink in the kitchen was lacking paper towels. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
Dishes were observed in the hand-washing sink at the bar. A hand-washing sink must not be used for purposes other than hand washing. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Shellstock tags are not maintained for 90 days. Shellstock tags or labels are required to be maintained for 90 calendar days from the date the container is emptied.PIC informed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (ice machine) were observed with an accumulation of biofilm. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch.PIC voluntarily disposed of ice. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Various food product was observed held at (50-53) degrees Fahrenheit. Time/temperature control for safety (TCS) food must be held at 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below except during preparation, cooking, or cooling.PIC voluntarily disposed of food product. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

The presence of rodents is not controlled as evidenced by droppings throughout the office. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Lowboy located in the servers prep area was not in good repair. Ambient temperature was 50DF. Establishment not allowed to store any TCS food product in unit until repaired/replaced. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024

Beverage tubing was observed in contact with ice used for drinks at the bar. Beverage tubing and cold plate beverage cooling devices must not be in contact with stored ice. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2024
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quaternary sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2024
The office has an accumulation of soil residue, rodent droppings and clutter. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/27/2024
